author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Session dedicated to understanding and unblocking the deployment architecture after GitHub Copilot's guidance had left Ameen confused about the direction. Copilot had asked for a Bedrock license (obtained), a SharePoint list (created), and a Teams app upload (attempted but failing). Worked through the full deployment plan: Flow 1 (SharePoint row triggers GitHub Actions via workflow_dispatch), Flow 2 (polls GitHub and updates SharePoint rows to Complete/Failed), and Flow 3 (uploads report files to SharePoint document library). Created an Alternative Deployment Plan .md file. Worked through AWS credentials setup (access key, secret key, session token as separate GitHub secrets) and troubleshot Power Automate connector issues including missing SharePoint Update Item connector, URI encoding errors, and invalid runAfter logic.
problems: Teams sideload upload was failing — unclear what was wrong with the app package
GitHub Copilot had given a confusing deployment path; needed to reframe the architecture
AWS credentials setup was unclear (three separate GitHub secrets required: access key ID, secret key, session token)
Power Automate flows were failing with missing SharePoint connectors, URI encoding issues, and invalid runAfter references
Flow 2 (status polling) needed clean end-to-end proof before declaring it done
Gap reports were GitHub Actions artifacts, not in SharePoint document library — blocking declarative agent from reading them
approaches: Clarified the full three-flow Power Automate architecture (Flow 1: trigger, Flow 2: status poll, Flow 3: report upload)
Created alternative_deployment_plan.md capturing the full plan including declarative agent as required (not optional)
Set up AWS credentials as three separate GitHub secrets
Diagnosed Power Automate connector failures: SharePoint Update Item and HTTP actions not available
Proposed Power Automate HTTP trigger as an alternative to direct SharePoint writes from GitHub Actions — no OAuth registration needed
results: Flow 1 confirmed working: new SharePoint rows trigger GitHub Actions and receive 204
Flow 2 built but not yet end-to-end proven — OutputFolder still blank pending completion
Identified that gap reports must move from GitHub artifacts to SharePoint document library before declarative agent can read them

decisions.decision: Declarative agent is required in the deployment plan, not optional
Use Power Automate HTTP trigger for Flow 3 to avoid OAuth registration and premium connectors
Test against personal SharePoint site first before the shared production site

open_questions: Why are standard GitHub-hosted runners not acquiring for the enterprise EMU repo?
What is the exact flow configuration needed to fix the runAfter reference error in Power Automate?
